meta.description: G25 TDA audit data — see audit_report.md for full text.

M1.name: Universal null (Bobrowski–Skraba)
M1.principle: Per-feature p-value from a Gumbel fit to the bulk of log(death/birth).
M1.reveals: fine-scale features whose scale-invariant signature is in the tail of the noise distribution

M2.name: Subsample bootstrap confidence band (Fasy et al.)
M2.principle: Resample the cloud B=50 times at size m=4000; bottleneck distance to the empirical diagram gives confidence radius c_α.
M2.reveals: features that survive any reasonable resampling of the dataset (only the absolute largest loops)

M3.name: DTM filtration (Chazal–Cohen-Steiner–Mérigot)
M3.principle: Wasserstein-stable filtration via distance-to-measure; same universal-null test on the resulting diagram.
M3.reveals: features that exist under a fundamentally different way of building the filtration (cross-filtration cross-check)

ancient.m3_consensus_check_note: M3 acts as a cross-filtration sanity check (DTM Wasserstein-stable filtration), not a direct intersection with M1∩M2. m3_supports is True iff the cloud has at least one BH-FDR-significant H_1 feature under M3 — a necessary but not sufficient condition for any specific M1∩M2 feature to 'pass all three methods'. A direct three-way intersection would require cocycle representatives from the DTM run, which gudhi.WeightedRipsComplex does not currently expose.
